hook.text: Cassini orbited Saturn for 13 years, transforming our understanding of the ringed planet and its moons.

buildup.text: It carried the Huygens probe, which landed on Titan in 2005—the farthest landing from Earth ever.

discovery.text: Cassini found water geysers erupting from Enceladus, hinting at a warm ocean beneath its icy crust.

twist.text: Titan has lakes and rain of liquid methane—the only other world with surface liquids.

climax.text: In 2017, Cassini plunged into Saturn to avoid contaminating moons that might harbor life.

punchline.text: Thirteen years at Saturn. A grand finale dive into the clouds.

quiz.question: Why was Cassini deliberately crashed into Saturn?
options: To avoid contaminating moons like Enceladus that might have life
To collect atmospheric samples during its final descent
Its fuel ran out and it could no longer maintain orbit
